description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a robust, gridded platform. The panels are arranged in two main sections, each tilted at an angle, and are supported by a series of dark, block-like structures at the corners and along the sides. The base platform is composed of a series of parallel slats or rails, likely for structural support and ventilation. There are visible raised elements and possible access points or maintenance hatches integrated into the platform. The color palette is predominantly black and gray, with some red accents visible at the edges, suggesting electrical or safety features. The overall design is industrial and utilitarian, suitable for urban deployment on flat surfaces such as rooftops, parking structures, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A large, industrial solar panel array with two tilted panel sections on a gridded black and gray platform, accented with red details and robust support structures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two main tilted solar panel sections and the central gridded platform, with block-like supports on the sides.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the elongated, slatted base structure, with visible red accents at the far end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the platform, the panel tilt from the opposite side, and additional support structures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angular orientation of the panels and the symmetry of the support blocks and base.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing toward the longer edge of the platform; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a robust steel and aluminum frame structure.
attribute_array_configuration: Two main rows of large panels, each row containing a single wide panel section, arranged parallel to each other on the platform.
